name: Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop
name_original: Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo
description: This moderate 25.2-mile mountain biking loop from Perales offers 744 feet of elevation gain and includes the Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoi
region_intro: Mountain biking around Perales offers routes primarily characterized by the flat and gently undulating terrain surrounding the Canal de Castilla. The landscape features open fields, canal paths, and occasional viewpoints, providing a mix of easy and moderate riding experiences. The area's low elevation gains make it accessible for various skill levels, with longer routes exploring the local countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Perales The most popular mountain bike trail is Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop, a 25.2 miles (40.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views of the surrounding area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop, a moderate 18.7 miles (30.1 km) path. This route follows the historic Canal de Castilla, providing a mostly flat and enjoyable ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Lock 33, Canal de Castilla – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Monzón de Campos, a 35.1 miles (56.5 km) trail leading through the canal landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Perales is defined by canal paths, open agricultural land, and gentle hills, with routes suitable for different ability levels. The network offers options for various skill levels, from easy family rides to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Perales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 38 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Perales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Perales?
A: There are over 9 mountain bike routes available around Perales, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Perales?
A: The mountain bike trails around Perales are primarily characterized by flat and gently undulating terrain, often following the historic Canal de Castilla. You'll encounter open fields, canal paths, and occasional viewpoints, making for a generally accessible and enjoyable ride.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Perales?
A: Yes, Perales offers several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. The terrain is generally flat with low elevation gains, especially along the canal paths, making it ideal for less experienced riders. There are 2 easy routes available.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders in Perales?
A: While many routes are moderate, there is at least one difficult mountain bike route available: &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks – Canal Entrance to Palencia loop from El Carrión&lt;/a&gt;. This 81.8 km trail involves more significant elevation changes compared to other routes in the area, offering a longer and more demanding ride.

Q: What are some popular mountain bike loop trails in Perales?
A: Popular loop trails include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/perales-and-villanueva-del-rebollar-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40.5 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364739930/canal-de-castilla-and-amayuelas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla and Amayuelas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 30.1 km path that follows the historic canal.

Q: What is the average length and duration of mountain bike trails in Perales?
A: Mountain bike trails in Perales vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/perales-and-villanueva-del-rebollar-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt; is 40.5 km and takes about 3 hours 14 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock 33, Canal de Castilla – Puente de Puentecillas loop from Monzón de Campos&lt;/a&gt; is 56.5 km and typically takes around 4 hours 25 minutes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Perales?
A: The routes in Perales are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 38 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the accessible canal paths and scenic views.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or places to see along the mountain bike trails in Perales?
A: Yes, several interesting landmarks can be found along or near the mountain bike routes. You might encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2615872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra de Ribas Locks&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4300031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Gallo&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR-89 Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; itself is a significant historical feature.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Perales?
A: Given the generally flat and open terrain, mountain biking in Perales is enjoyable across many seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and often beautiful scenery with blooming fields or changing foliage. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter rides are also possible, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there any scenic mountain bike trails in Perales?
A: Many trails in Perales offer scenic views, particularly those that follow the Canal de Castilla. The routes provide picturesque vistas of open agricultural land, the tranquil canal, and the surrounding countryside, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365693471/perales-and-villanueva-del-rebollar-viewpoint-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perales and Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical trail conditions for mountain biking in Perales?
A: The trails in Perales primarily consist of canal paths and tracks through open agricultural land. These are generally well-maintained and suitable for mountain biking. Conditions can vary with weather, with some sections potentially becoming muddy after rain, but overall, they offer a smooth and accessible riding experience.
tips: This small town of only about 50 residents is surrounded by hills with open fields and many great dirt trails. In the town you can visit the Church of San Juan Bautista or the alleys. Just outside the town, where the road changes to dirt, is the Hermitage of Our Lady of Arbas.

Here, at the top of the hill, with giant windmills all around, you have a spectacular view of the countryside below. This is the highest point in the area so it is easy to see for miles and miles. There are some information signs that explain the area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The 'Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo' mountain biking route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 14 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is graded as 'moderate' in difficulty. While it's not considered extremely challenging, beginners should be comfortable with mountain biking on varied terrain and be prepared for a distance of over 40 kilometers and some elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Specific local conditions for this trail are not detailed, but generally, the best times for mountain biking in this region of Spain are spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are usually milder, and the weather is more stable than in the peak summer heat or winter cold.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a couple of interesting points. You'll have the opportunity to see the Hermitage of Our Lady of Arbas and enjoy views from the Villanueva del Rebollar Viewpoint.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact trail are not available, many mountain biking trails in Spain are dog-friendly. If you bring your dog, it's always best practice to keep them on a leash, especially near wildlife or other trail users, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the 'Mirador de Villanueva del Rebollar desde Perales — circular por Campo de vuelo Villoldo' mountain biking route. Most natural trails in Spain are freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Specific details about the terrain for this particular route are not readily available. However, mountain biking trails in the broader region often feature a mix of natural singletrack, unpaved roads, and potentially some challenging sections with loose surfaces or rocky patches. Expect varied surfaces typical of rural Spanish landscapes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Perales. While specific parking areas are not detailed, you would typically look for suitable public parking within or on the outskirts of Perales to begin your ride. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route follow parts of several official trails. You will ride along portions of the Cañada Real Leonesa Oriental, the Camino de Villada, and the Camino de Calzada de los Molinos a Riberos de la Cueza.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages such as Perales, Villanueva del Rebollar, and Villoldo. You may find local cafes or restaurants in these settlements, particularly in Perales where the route starts, for refreshments before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and distance of over 40 km, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), appropriate cycling attire, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. A helmet is essential for safety.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Arbas
